body: In this screenshot we see that: - Animation gradient does not span 2 paragraphs/steps of thinking. No it looks broken, because each paragraph/line/step has its own gradient. The gradient is to create a feeling that there is more content, so it should be applied to full scrolled container of all thinking steps. - Thinking steps are not fully written, some parts are omitted. - When where is no yet message body and only thinking steps we the width of message broken, that causes bad feeling, as UI have sudden jump in width when message starts displaying. - We still have theming issues in all `services` box - Not all buttons on top menu are reacting to hover as other buttons, so it is partial. We need to double check all the UI issues (even out of scope of that I described), if the issue is one place it should be fixed in all places. So all our UI are have uniform behavior, we should reuse our own react.js components to easily support everything. And must fully transition to https://chakra-ui.com and JSX, so we can ensure everything is nice and polished. We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions). If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration. If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code. Also double check to fully apply requirements to entire codebase, so if we have issue in multiple places, it should be fixed in all them. Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done.

comments.body:
## Deep analysis — root causes, reproductions, workarounds & fixes

I've root-caused all five reported defects plus the codebase-wide consistency
requirements. The fix is up in **#551**, and a full case study (timeline, every
requirement, per-problem root causes, solution plans, library/Chakra research, and
before/after renders) is compiled at
[`docs/case-studies/issue-1963/`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/tree/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963)
in the tracking repo.

### Why these exist (one common thread)

None is a fresh bug — each is a residual rough edge from features shipped quickly
across #488 (the thinking preview) and #541/#549 (the theme pass + the desktop
auto-update panel). The thread connecting problems 4 and 5: `src/web/styles.css` uses
**zero CSS custom properties**. Light and dark are *manually duplicated hex* across
three layers (light base, `:root[data-theme="dark"]`, `@media (prefers-color-scheme:
dark)`), and each interactive element is styled by hand — so every new surface or
button silently misses rules the older ones already have.

---

### Problem 1 — the reasoning fade is per-line, not per-container

**Reproduce.** Open a message with ≥2 collapsed thinking steps. Each step line
carries its own gradient → two separate fades instead of one continuous "more below".

**Root cause.** The `mask-image` gradient is applied to the per-line
`.thinking-preview-previous`, not to the scrolling container.

**Workaround (none needed for users; for devs).** Temporarily set
`-webkit-mask-image: none` on `.thinking-preview-previous` to confirm the per-line
mask is the culprit.

**Fix (in #551).** Move the gradient to the container and scope it with `:has()` so a
lone first step stays fully legible:
```css
.thinking-preview-collapsed:has(.thinking-preview-previous) {
  -webkit-mask-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent 0, #000 1.4em);
          mask-image: linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent 0, #000 1.4em);
}
/* remove the per-line mask on .thinking-preview-previous */
```

---

### Problem 2 — thinking steps are clipped mid-sentence

**Reproduce.** Emit a step whose detail (e.g. a pasted prompt or composed answer) is
> 120 characters; it is cut with an ellipsis, reading as "omitted".

**Root cause.** Detail is capped at **120 chars** in *two* parallel constants:
`truncate_thinking_detail` (`src/thinking.rs`) and `thinkingDetailText`
(`src/web/app.js`).

**Workaround.** Keep individual step details short (< 120 chars). Not viable for
genuinely long single steps.

**Fix (in #551).** Raise the cap to **600** in *both* surfaces, kept in sync (a
pathological over-cap detail still truncates so the preview stays bounded):
```rust
// src/thinking.rs
let limit = 600; // was 120
```
```js
// src/web/app.js
if (chars.length <= 600) return text;          // was 120
return `${chars.slice(0, 599).join("").trimEnd()}…`;
```

---

### Problem 3 — pending message jumps in width

**Reproduce.** Watch a message that shows only thinking steps (no body yet): the body
renders at a narrow ~116px, then snaps to full width when the answer streams in.

**Root cause.** A leftover typing-indicator rule pins the pending body:
```css
.pending .message-body { width: 116px } /* relic of the old typing-dots indicator */
```

**Workaround.** None at runtime; it's a layout rule.

**Fix (in #551).** Remove the fixed-width clamp (and the dead `.typing::after`) so the
pending body uses the same width as a settled body — nothing to jump.

---

### Problem 4 — the `services` box ignores the dark theme

**Reproduce.** Switch to dark mode and open the desktop **services**/**update**
panel: cards, the token input, action buttons and the "Update available" status
render with light surfaces.

**Root cause.** When those panels were added, only the light base rules were written;
the dark counterparts in `:root[data-theme="dark"]` and the `prefers-color-scheme`
media query were never hand-duplicated, so they fall through to light styling.

**Workaround.** None for users.

**Fix (in #551).** Add complete dark rules for **every** services/update surface in
**both** dark layers (the explicit `data-theme` override and the
`prefers-color-scheme` fallback), e.g.:
```css
:root[data-theme="dark"] .desktop-service { background: #1b2420; border-color: #2c3a34; color: #e7f0ec; }
/* …dot, state, token input, url, actions/update-actions (+hover), start/install, update-state, services-error… */
@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
  :root:not([data-theme="light"]) .desktop-service { /* same */ }
}
```
**Systemic fix (recommended next):** introduce CSS custom properties
(`--surface`, `--border`, `--text-muted`, …) so a new surface *inherits* correct
theming instead of re-deriving hex. This removes the duplication that caused this
class of bug — and is the natural first step of the Chakra migration below.

---

### Problem 5 — only some top-bar buttons react to hover

**Reproduce.** Hover each header control: some highlight, others are inert.

**Root cause.** Top-bar controls were styled piecemeal; newer buttons
(`report`/`source-code`/`download`/`memory`, `sidebar-toggle`, `mobile-menu-toggle`)
never got the older ones' `:hover` rules. No shared treatment.

**Workaround.** None for users.

**Fix (in #551).** Give every top-bar control one shared hover + active-hover +
keyboard `:focus-visible` ring, in light **and** dark:
```css
.report-button:hover, .source-code-button:hover, .download-button:hover,
.memory-button:hover, .sidebar-toggle:hover, .mobile-menu-toggle:hover,
.mode-toggle:hover, .diagnostics-toggle:hover { border-color: #86b8a7; background: #edf7f3; }
/* + a shared :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid … } */
```

---

### Codebase-wide & Chakra (meta-requirements)

* **"Fix it in all places."** Problem 2 is fixed in *both* runtimes (Rust + JS);
  problems 4 and 5 in *both* dark layers.
* **Chakra UI + JSX migration.** Documented as a staged, multi-PR migration in
  [`solution-plans.md`](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1963-d2c252441bed/docs/case-studies/issue-1963/solution-plans.md):
  (1) introduce CSS design tokens (removes the problem-4/5 root cause, no visual
  change), (2) add a JSX toolchain producing the same committed bundles, (3) add a
  Chakra provider whose semantic tokens are those design tokens, (4) port leaf
  components in reviewable slices (topbar `IconButton` folds in problem 5; services
  cards fold in problem 4; thinking preview folds in problems 1/3). It is the right
  strategic direction but disproportionate to bundle with five cosmetic fixes, so
  #551 fixes the defects in a token-ready way the migration can absorb.

### Tests (in #551)

* `tests/unit/issue_1963.rs` — pins the new 600-char bound (full detail survives;
  over-cap still truncates).
* `tests/e2e/tests/issue-1963.spec.js` — 10 Playwright tests for problems 1/3/4/5
  (single continuous mask, pending-width parity, dark computed colors, hover/focus
  across every top-bar control). They fail 9/10 when the CSS fix is stashed.

Fix PR: **#551**.

title: Unexpected UI/UX behavior
